evidence_summary: Using character-level information can improve vector representations for morphologically rich languages.
Representing words as the sum of character n-gram vectors can be an effective approach.
Character-level embeddings can reduce the number of parameters in the input layer and allow the model to consume arbitrary words.
Subword modeling can enable open-vocabulary translation by encoding rare and unknown words as sequences of subword units.
